commit.message:
fix: ChatWidget polling istf Supabase Realtime (ingen supabase-dep i admin)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SH assistant website flow — Telegram bridge + Supabase Realtime

- request/route.ts: skickar uppgifter via Telegram Bot API till clawdbot-sh
  (WebSocket fungerar ej från Vercel serverless)
- request/route.ts: sparar tasks i Supabase istf in-memory Map
- status/route.ts: läser från Supabase istf mock/in-memory
- callback/route.ts: uppdaterar clawdbot_tasks → triggar Realtime
- ChatWidget.tsx: Supabase Realtime subscription — öppnar och notifierar
  automatiskt när hemsida är klar. Tar bort VPS-statuskoll (alltid Online)
- 016_clawdbot_tasks.sql: ny tabell för persistent task-tracking + Realtime

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Respect GPU minimum fan speed hardware limit

Queries nvmlDeviceGetMinMaxFanSpeed per GPU and enforces the floor
everywhere: curve drag, text entries, manual slider, and the poll
loop that writes to hardware. Draws an orange dashed 'min N%' line
on the curve canvas so the limit is visible.

commit.message:
Add Table, Tabs, Input, Select, useInterval, useTimeout, useBacklinks, useFileContent, usePlugin

UI Components:
- Table: sortable, searchable, selectable, paginated with custom column renderers
- Tabs: underline and pills variants with active state
- Input: themed text/number input with label
- Select: themed dropdown with label, supports string[] or {label, value}[]

Timer Hooks:
- useInterval(callback, ms) — auto-cleanup, pass null to pause
- useTimeout(callback, ms) — auto-cleanup, pass null to cancel

Vault Hooks:
- useBacklinks() — returns notes linking to the current note
- useFileContent(path) — reads file with live updates on modification
- usePlugin(pluginId) — access any installed plugin's API

7 new tests (74 total), example page 16-ui-components.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle nil values in Redis Hmget to prevent panic (#89)

When HMGet returns nil for non-existent fields, the type assertion
result.(string) would panic. This fix checks for nil values and
returns empty strings instead, maintaining the array length while
avoiding runtime panics.

Fixes #22

Add apiKey parameter to GetIPAddress function to allow users to provide

abused and violate security best practices.

commit.message:
Feat: Botones recuperar foto/datos

- `TarjetaEscaneo`: botones recuperar foto y datos aparecen solo cuando el usuario modifica algo respecto al original de la API/BD (`fotoModificada`, `datosModificados` computeds). Fix de orden de declaración que causaba zona temporal muerta.
- `TarjetaProductoBorrador`: misma lógica aplicada en la Mesa de Trabajo. `datosOriginales` leído desde `props.item.datosOriginales` (snapshot guardado por el store). Botón recuperar foto al lado de la cámara; recuperar datos al extremo derecho. Íconos de cámara aumentados a `size="md"`.
- `SelectorComercioDireccion`: fix ESLint `props` declarado pero no usado.
- `PlanMesaTrabajo.md`: fases 1, 2 y 3 marcadas como completadas; sección 3.4 agregada; fase 4 actualizada con ítems completados.

- PostForWWWFormForBytres
- PostForWWWFormForAll

in long-running applications.

Also add comprehensive unit tests for the affected functions.

commit.message:
fix: configure errcheck exclusions for standard unactionable patterns

golangci-lint v2 flags deferred Close() and os.RemoveAll() calls by
default (stricter than v1). These are standard Go idioms where errors
are genuinely not actionable:
- (io.Closer).Close: defer conn/body/file Close() — idiomatic cleanup
- os.RemoveAll: temp dir cleanup — failure doesn't affect correctness
- fmt.Fprint*: terminal writes — can't report errors to a broken terminal

commit.message:
Switch Studio to patch-based approach: fixes AI destroying pages

Instead of asking the AI to rewrite 300+ lines of JSX (which always
fails — removing content, breaking layouts, inventing text), the AI
now outputs a JSON array of className find-and-replace patches.

The client applies these patches to the ORIGINAL code, making it
structurally impossible to:
- Remove sections, cards, or content
- Change text, headings, or labels
- Reorder or restructure the layout
- Invent new content or hero banners

The AI can ONLY modify Tailwind className strings — the exact kind
of targeted CSS improvement the Studio was meant to provide.

Also lowered temperature from 0.6 to 0.3 for more precise patches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: survive WebSocket reconnects during long-running Claude Code sessions

Previously, when the WS connection dropped during orchestration, build-app,
or one-off tasks, the running session's output vanished because it held a
stale client reference with a dead socket. Now:

- Frontend sends a persistent clientId (sessionStorage UUID) on every WS connect
- Backend swaps ws/send on the existing ConnectedClient object on reconnect,
  so all captured references automatically route to the new socket
- Disconnected clients get a 60-second grace period before cleanup
- One-off task router now passes targetCardId to runClaudeCode so output
  renders in a proper terminal card instead of a chat bubble

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(pipeline): truncate book_id to fit Vectorize 64-byte ID limit (#15)

Cloudflare Vectorize limits vector IDs to 64 bytes. Since chunk_id
is "{book_id}:{index:05d}" (6-byte suffix), book_id must be at most
58 bytes. Add max_length parameter to slugify() to enforce this.

Also add error response logging for non-retryable Vectorize errors.
